body:
## Verdict (LIVE-CORRECTED)

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable  (DOC-based coverage said `full` with the note "step-finish reason=error covers"; the live store inspection overrode it)
driver_capability: ready

## Scenario

token-quota-exhausted: a token/quota budget is exhausted (provider 429 / cost cap / context-window overflow); the model call fails and aborts the turn. User-observable signal per the scenario `verify`: min_turns≥1, final_state `ready`, has_waiting_state false — i.e. the session settles to `ready` after the quota failure and does NOT stick in `working`, with no `waiting` transition.

## Why this degrades to applicable_false (observability gap)

This is the SAME transport/observability gap already proven for the opencode/turn-aborted-by-error sibling: a quota/token-budget exhaustion is just one error class of an error-aborted turn, and opencode records ALL turn aborts on the PARENT MESSAGE row (`message.data.error`), never as a `step-finish` part.

The doc-based coverage recorded daemon_capability=full on the reasoning that opencode's parser maps reason=error → turn_done (parser.go:92, `case "error"`) and the watcher's isTerminalPart accepts a step-finish part with reason in {stop, interrupted, length, error, content-filter} (watcher.go:435). That parser/watcher branch is real — but opencode 1.14.50 NEVER WRITES a step-finish part with reason=error on a quota/error-aborted turn.

Empirical evidence, gathered live against ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db (opencode 1.14.50):

1. Store-wide audit of every step-finish part ever persisted: reason=stop(102), reason=tool-calls(165), and ZERO with reason=error. The only part types in the store are reasoning/step-finish/step-start/text/tool — none carries a quota/rate-limit/error signal.
2. opencode records the quota/error abort on the message row, not as a part: message.data.error rows = APIError(10), MessageAbortedError(26), UnknownError(3). The actual messages include the closest live token-budget analog — context-window overflow: UnknownError "The number of tokens to keep from the initial prompt is greater than the context length (n_keep: 27429 >= n_ctx: 4096)" — plus quota/auth-class APIErrors ("Unauthorized: Invalid LLMGateway API token", "Failed to load model … insufficient system resources", "User not found"). All land on message.data.error.
3. The watcher's scanParts query (watcher.go:456-463) JOINs `message` ONLY to obtain role context and then explicitly discards it (`_ = msgData`, watcher.go:498); the terminal-turn decision (watcher.go:495) depends solely on isTerminalPart(partData). So a quota turn produces a user `text` part → session goes `working`, then an assistant `message` with `error` set and NO step-finish part → no `turn_done` is emitted → the session sticks in `working` until `process_exited`. There is no working-state idle sweep (same gap noted for the codex sibling). The scenario's user-observable signal — settle to `ready` after the quota failure — is therefore NOT observable from the store.

## Driver angle is moot

The send-only opencode driver (drive-opencode-interactive.sh: send/sleep/wait_turn) is `ready`, and a context-overflow prompt against the local gemma DOES deterministically induce a token-budget error live. But the error lands on message.data.error with no step-finish, so even a perfect driver cannot make the daemon observe the working→ready settle. The claudecode mock-anthropic-429 path does NOT transfer either: that recorder launches `claude --bare` with ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointed at an Anthropic-only stub; opencode has no `--bare` equivalent and routes through its own config-resolved providers, so there is no opencode-side 429 mock. This is therefore an observability gap, NOT a driver_gap.

## Recipe disposition

by_adapter.opencode set to applicable:false with a scope_note; no recipe, no recording, no expected.jsonl authored (a spec phase asserting working→ready after the quota failure would be unrecordable). observability_correction flagged so the maintainer flips the coverage cell daemon_capability full→incapable (and drops the now-falsified "step-finish reason=error covers" note).

## Unblocks when

EITHER (a) opencode emits a `step-finish` part with reason=error on quota/error-aborted turns (so isTerminalPart fires), OR (b) the opencode watcher/parser consumes `message.data.error` as a terminal-turn signal and synthesizes a turn_done (mirroring aider's flushErrorTurn epilogue, the #262 path). Either change would let the existing parser branch resolve working→ready and make this cell recordable via a context-overflow prompt through the send-only driver.
caveats: DOC-vs-LIVE divergence: the doc-based coverage (daemon_capability=full, "step-finish reason=error covers") was overridden by the live store inspection. The parser branch (case "error" → turn_done) exists but is dead for opencode quota/error turns because the upstream step-finish reason=error part is never persisted.

Not a driver gap: this is a transport/observability gap identical to the opencode/turn-aborted-by-error sibling. Even with a context-overflow trigger the send-only driver elicits live (n_keep > n_ctx), no step-finish reason=error part is written, so the daemon cannot observe the turn-end. Hence applicable_false, not driver_gap.

Probed on opencode 1.14.50 with the default local model lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored (LM Studio). Store-wide audit of ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db: step-finish reasons stop(102)/tool-calls(165)/error(0); message.data.error rows APIError(10)/MessageAbortedError(26)/UnknownError(3) incl. context-overflow n_keep>n_ctx.

UPDATE 2026-05-26: same as turn-aborted-by-error — daemon now reads message.data.error (watcher.go isErrorMessage, unit-tested), so daemon_capability is full; recording still blocked by the parts-only transcript export (gap:error-export). Tracked #493.

sources.note: parseStepFinish maps reason=error → turn_done (line 92 comment, parseStepFinish at 72/88) — but this branch only fires when a step-finish part with reason=error is present, which opencode does not write on quota/error-aborted turns.

isTerminalPart (435) accepts reason in {stop,interrupted,length,error,content-filter}; scanParts (456-463) JOINs message only for role context and discards it (`_ = msgData`, line 498), so the terminal decision (line 495) never reads message.data.error as a terminal signal.

Live store audit (opencode 1.14.50): step-finish reasons = stop(102), tool-calls(165), error(0); part types = reasoning/step-finish/step-start/text/tool only; message.data.error = APIError(10)/MessageAbortedError(26)/UnknownError(3), incl. context-overflow 'n_keep > context length'. Confirms quota/error turns persist at message level, never as a step-finish part.
